articles.headline: Alphabet prices record $85 billion AI-focused stock sale
articles.summary: Alphabet’s record stock offering signals sustained investor appetite for AI infrastructure and suggests continued heavy spending on compute, data centers, and related supply chains. For businesses, this can translate into higher AI capacity availability over time, but also stronger competition for cloud and infrastructure resources as hyperscalers keep scaling.

articles.headline: OpenAI unveils Lockdown Mode to protect sensitive data from prompt injection attacks
articles.summary: A dedicated defense against prompt injection suggests AI security is becoming a core enterprise requirement rather than an optional feature. For businesses deploying AI assistants on internal data, this raises the importance of access controls, policy enforcement, and vendor due diligence before rollout.

articles.headline: Weekly Intelligence Report highlights ransomware, vulnerabilities, and data leaks
articles.summary: CYFIRMA’s weekly report describes ransomware and leak activity affecting a transportation and logistics company in Singapore and a construction company in Japan. Those sectors are operationally important for many businesses, so the report is a reminder that supply-chain disruption and third-party cyber risk remain immediate business concerns.[5]
